purpose: Promote older, siloed, and frozen local tools into explicit Project Telos flagship lanes without copying private payloads or bypassing receipt gates.

contract.privacy_boundary: Interop requires tool identity, capability summaries, hashes, source refs, promotion status, risk boundaries, and next actions. Raw private corpora, secrets, credentials, private client data, and purchased font files are not required.

tools.name: Calibrate Pro
tools.lineage: Frozen display calibration application with color math, panel workflows, ICC/LUT output, and verification reports.

evidence: README describes a Windows display-calibration toolkit for sensorless profiles, measured workflows, ICC/3D-LUT output, and verification reports.
Calibrate Pro README depends on Quanta Color for color-science primitives.

tools.risk_boundary: Display calibration and color measurement only; monitor/profile changes should stay local, reversible, and user-visible, with artifact hashes used for interop.
next_actions: Expose a read-only telos.display.calibration contract before wiring live monitor controls.
Route generated color packets through Crucible measurement gates.
Use Quanta Color as the shared color-science library.

tools.name: Quanta Color
tools.lineage: Color-science workbench for color spaces, HDR transfer functions, perceptual differences, ICC profiles, and gamut workflows.

evidence: README describes color spaces, tone mapping, HDR, color appearance models, delta-E metrics, adaptation, spectral rendering, ICC, CVD simulation, and gamut mapping.

tools.risk_boundary: Color math library only; no device mutation or private asset export is required for host interop.
next_actions: Define a compact color packet shared by Calibrate Pro, Telos Studio, and Crucible.
Expose deterministic delta-E and gamut checks through measurement layers.

tools.name: QuantaLang / quantac
tools.lineage: Compiled effects language and quantac compiler lineage for C, HLSL, GLSL, and future shader/runtime targets.

evidence: README describes quantac compiling .quanta programs to C, HLSL, GLSL, and experimental runtime targets.

tools.risk_boundary: Effects-language work must compile to inspectable artifacts and avoid hidden payloads, network side effects, or opaque generated code.
next_actions: Inventory runnable compiler tests before importing code into Telos.
Create a shader/effect recipe adapter that emits receipts and source maps.
Keep generated artifacts deterministic enough for Crucible checks.

tools.name: WARDEN Security Lineage
tools.lineage: Older modular C++23 security assessment and training platform lineage for defensive analysis, synthetic labs, and good-faith fix workflows.

evidence: README describes a modular security assessment/training platform with tests and broad module coverage.

tools.risk_boundary: defensive, authorized, good-faith OSS patching and synthetic training only. No live offensive automation, credential collection, stealth behavior, or exploit publication is exposed by this registry.
next_actions: Extract only defensive analyzers, fixtures, and receipt patterns.
Route public OSS bug-crawl output through maintainer-friendly reports and disclosure-safe patches.
Keep any dual-use material quarantined until adapted into defensive tests.

tools.name: Agent Audit
tools.lineage: Public AI-agent session audit utility for comparing declared intent, action ledgers, and scope policy.

evidence: README describes declared intents, action logs, policy checks, drift detection, and reports for human review.

tools.risk_boundary: Synthetic and local review loops only; no private operational data is required for promotion.
next_actions: Convert loop-ledger entries into agent-audit inputs.
Feed audit results into Crucible as evidence packets.

tools.name: Context Curator Lite
tools.lineage: Public context-curation utility for token-efficient planning fragments, heuristic redaction, and Telos context-envelope export.
capabilities: relative source refs and content hashes

evidence: README describes sanitized context extraction, heuristic redaction, relative source references, and Project Telos context-envelope export for large-workspace agent work.

tools.risk_boundary: Context compression only; it is not a security boundary, does not copy raw transcripts, and emits compact summaries that are authoritative only through source refs and hashes.
next_actions: Use --telos-envelope when preparing unattended large-workspace handoffs.
Route exported envelopes through Index context packs and Crucible verification gates.
Attach downstream test receipts before treating implementation claims as proven.

tools.name: Secret Redact IO
tools.lineage: Public guarded IO utility for redacted file, fetch, write, and subprocess channels with hash-only receipts.

evidence: README describes guarded reads, writes, fetches, subprocess execution, redaction, and hash-only receipts.

tools.risk_boundary: Guardrail utility only; receipts store hashes and redaction counts, not raw secrets.
next_actions: Use as the default safe IO substrate for Gather and Index adapters.
Add receipt compatibility tests against Telos action receipts.

tools.name: Repo Proof Index
tools.lineage: Public proof-packet indexer for reviewer-ready visibility over contracts, receipts, backend descriptors, and evidence gaps.

evidence: README describes indexing proof artifacts, receipt bundles, witness receipts, backend descriptors, summaries, and research harnesses.

tools.risk_boundary: Indexes evidence and action items; it does not certify truth or read private payloads referenced by a contract.
next_actions: Use as the proof visibility layer for multi-repo promotion sweeps.
Emit Telos-compatible proof packets for each flagship release surface.

tools.name: Release Surface Scanner
tools.lineage: Public release packet scanner for required files, env exclusion, secret-shape detection, hashes, and proof index output.

evidence: README describes required release files, .env exclusion, secret-shape detection, file hashing, reports, and proof index generation.

tools.risk_boundary: Release assurance only; it must not output secrets, private operator data, private evidence, client data, credentials, or .env files.
next_actions: Run before every flagship publish or PR demonstration.
Feed release reports into Repo Proof Index and Crucible review.

tools.name: GPU Trace Validator
tools.lineage: Public GPU trace JSON validator for bounded, redacted rendering and compute evidence receipts.

evidence: README describes validating GPU trace JSON against bundled schema files and emitting bounded, redacted receipts.

tools.risk_boundary: Validates fixtures produced elsewhere; it does not capture GPU work or certify trust verdicts by itself.
next_actions: Define a Telos GPU trace packet for WebGPU/WebGL/canvas fallbacks.
Use Crucible measurement gates for assertion summaries.

tools.name: raw-native
tools.lineage: Local-only zero-dependency C++23 two-way rendering engine with G-buffer, ray-traced AO oracle, SSAO approximation, reconcile certificates, and bounded arena allocation.

evidence: README describes a zero-dependency C++23 renderer that compares SSAO against ray-traced AO and emits witnessed certificates plus bounded allocator certificates.

tools.risk_boundary: Original local renderer only; no proprietary game runtime, injected graphics API hook, private asset, or third-party shader payload is required for Telos interop.
next_actions: Run CMake and ctest before runtime adoption.
Wrap certificates as Telos rendering measurement packets.
Use Crucible measurement gates for oracle-vs-approximation verdicts.

tools.name: studio-libs
tools.lineage: Local-only zero-dependency nD rendering and perception libraries plus a native MCP organ for render, perceive, and measure operations.

evidence: README describes render-nd, sense-core, and a Node-stdlib MCP server for perceive, render, measure, and a live resource.

tools.risk_boundary: Native perception and rendering organ only; it is not weights-level ambient cognition and does not decide when a host should invoke it.
next_actions: Add a Telos MCP launch profile for studio-perception-mcp.
Adapt render-nd and sense-core packets into Telos scene and measurement envelopes.
Add reduced-motion and accessibility checks for visual outputs.

tools.name: Forum archive
tools.lineage: Local-only older Forum stdlib core for supervision-tree multi-agent systems with hashing, events, tasks, agents, supervision, and scheduling.

evidence: README describes a clean-room, zero-external-dependency Python engine for accountable supervision-tree multi-agent systems.

tools.risk_boundary: Archive import only; diff concepts against current Forum before moving code so old scheduler semantics do not regress current ledger behavior.
next_actions: Index and diff against current Forum routing and ledger code.
Promote only primitives that improve current route replay or bounded scheduling.
Record any import as a Forum ledger compatibility decision.

roadmap.focus: Keep every frozen or siloed tool visible with receipts, owner boundaries, and a flagship host.
Wrap promotion-ready tools through CLI JSON and MCP adapters before moving shared code.
Route color, calibration, effects-language, proof, release, and defensive find-and-fix packets through the five flagship loop.
Promote tools to runtime dependencies only after tests, receipts, docs, and privacy boundaries are stable.
